text.title: react-hooks Failure Modes
text.summary: Four high-frequency hook antipatterns the spec must never produce.

antipattern.symptom: Counter starts at 0, increments to 1 on the first tick, then stays at 1 forever. UI shows wrong values; tests pass on first render but fail on assertions after timer fires.
antipattern.root-cause: setInterval captured the initial render's `count` (= 0). The effect's [] deps prevented re-binding, so the captured closure stays at 0 forever; setCount(0 + 1) writes 1 every tick.
antipattern.fix: Use the functional updater: setCount(prev => prev + 1). Add a rule_ref="r4-functional-updater" entry in state_slices. ESLint exhaustive-deps will also suggest adding count to deps, but the functional updater is preferred for setIntervals.

antipattern.symptom: "React has detected a change in the order of Hooks called" runtime error; component crashes after a prop toggles.
antipattern.root-cause: A useState / useEffect is wrapped in `if (prop)` or sits after an `if (!enabled) return null;` early return. The hook count differs between renders, breaking React's slot-indexed state model.
antipattern.fix: Move all hook calls to the top of the function body, before any early return or conditional. Move the conditional into the JSX or into the effect body, not around the hook call. Exception: use() in React 19 may be called conditionally.

antipattern.symptom: The effect re-fires on every render, often producing an infinite render loop. React DevTools shows the same component re-rendering forever.
antipattern.root-cause: An object or array literal is passed as a useEffect dep: useEffect(() => {...}, [{a, b}]). Each render creates a new reference; Object.is comparison returns false; effect re-runs; effect updates state; new render; repeat.
antipattern.fix: Destructure to primitive deps: [a, b]. If the object MUST be passed somewhere, memoize it with useMemo and put the memoized reference in the deps. Add an ESLint exhaustive-deps lint rule that flags inline objects.

antipattern.symptom: The component still re-renders just as often; benchmarks show no improvement, but the code is now harder to read and review.
antipattern.root-cause: Every value, including cheap arithmetic and string concatenation, was wrapped in useMemo/useCallback. The hook overhead (allocation + comparison) is larger than the value's computation cost, so memoization is a pure tax.
antipattern.fix: Apply rule r6-memo-after-profile. Run the Profiler in dev, identify the slow component, only then memoize. Justify each memoization entry with a profiling fact. Enable React Compiler if available.
